items.question: "Where do you live?" → She asked where ___.
explanations: Reported questions drop the auxiliary 'do'.
Correct! Statement order + backshift: 'She asked where I lived'.
'Did I live' keeps question word order.
'I live' isn't backshifted after 'asked'.

items.question: "Are you ready?" → He asked ___ ready.
explanations: Correct! Yes/no questions take 'if' + statement order: 'He asked if I was ready'.
'Was I' keeps question word order.
'If was I' has the words in the wrong order.
'Am I' isn't backshifted and keeps question order.

items.question: "What is your name?" → She asked what ___.
explanations: 'Is' should backshift and follow statement order.
Correct! Statement order + backshift: 'She asked what my name was'.
'Was my name' keeps question word order.
'Did my name' makes no sense.

items.question: "Do you like coffee?" → He asked ___ coffee.
explanations: Correct! Yes/no questions take 'if' + backshift: 'He asked if I liked coffee'.
'Do I like' keeps the auxiliary and question order.
'Did I like' keeps question order.
'If did I like' wrongly keeps 'did'.

items.question: "Why did she leave?" → They asked why ___.
explanations: 'Did she leave' keeps question order.
Correct! Statement order + backshift: 'They asked why she had left'.
A reported question has no question mark.
'She leaves' isn't the backshift of 'did leave'.

items.question: "Can you swim?" → She asked ___ swim.
explanations: Correct! Yes/no + backshift: 'She asked if I could swim'.
'Can I' keeps question order and doesn't backshift.
'If can I' has wrong order.
'Could I' keeps question order (subject after modal).

items.question: "What are you doing?" → He asked what ___.
explanations: 'Are you doing' keeps question order.
Correct! Statement order + backshift: 'He asked what I was doing'.
'Was I doing' keeps question order.
'Am I doing' isn't backshifted.

items.question: Correct this: 'She asked me where did I work.'
options: She asked me where I worked.
She asked me where did I work.
She asked me where I did work.
She asked me where do I work.
explanations: Correct! Drop 'did', use statement order + backshift: 'where I worked'.
'Where did I work' keeps question order.
'Where I did work' still keeps the auxiliary.
'Where do I work' keeps the auxiliary and present tense.

items.question: "Have you seen my keys?" → He asked ___ his keys.
explanations: Correct! Yes/no + backshift: 'He asked if I had seen his keys'.
'Have I seen' keeps question order.
'Did I see' changes the tense wrongly.
'If have I seen' has wrong order.

items.question: Which reported question is correct?
options: She asked when the film started.
She asked when did the film start.
She asked when the film started?
She asked when does the film start.
explanations: Correct! Statement order, backshift, no question mark: 'She asked when the film started'.
This keeps question word order.
This wrongly adds a question mark.
This keeps the auxiliary 'does' and present tense.
